moduleDefinitions.name: Audit Hub Experience
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own the complete responsive, accessible Audit Hub front end: workspace selection, Overview, all nine lifecycle-area views and phase subviews, global search, dossiers, trace navigation, baseline comparison, findings, reviews, packages, sample reset, and refresh status.

moduleDefinitions.name: Workspace & Snapshot Management
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own project configuration, objective-profile references, repository definitions, real-versus-sample selection, last-real-project preference, exact revision sets, immutable snapshot identity, baselines, and publication state.

moduleDefinitions.name: Lifecycle Explorer
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own the phase model, phase-specific subviews, readiness summaries, evidence-table projections, compare projections, and shared lifecycle-area anatomy for Overview, Planning, Requirements, Design, Implementation, Verification, Configuration Management, Quality Assurance, and Certification.

moduleDefinitions.name: Evidence Graph
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own normalized lifecycle artifact metadata, typed relationships, requirement-to-result chains, provenance and hashes, dossiers, cross-phase search, staleness evaluation, change impact, and trace traversal.

moduleDefinitions.name: Assurance Workflow
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own deterministic and human findings, review and independence records, assignment, disposition, corrective action, reverification, closure history, configurable objective mappings, audit-package membership, manifests, and watermark policy.

moduleDefinitions.name: Ingestion & Publication
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own scanning, artifact classification, extractor orchestration, normalization, diagnostics, deterministic check execution, refresh progress, reliability gates, and atomic publication while preserving the last valid snapshot.

moduleDefinitions.name: Deterministic Sample Workspace
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Own the bundled AeroNav lateral-guidance normalized snapshot, baselines 2.3.0 and 2.4.0, seeded complete and incomplete chains, twelve findings, deterministic identities and hashes, resettable overlay defaults, and offline fallback behavior.

moduleDefinitions.name: Evidence Store
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Persist and query project metadata, immutable snapshots, normalized evidence, graph relationships, search and phase projections, reviews, findings, activity history, sample overlay state, comparison inputs, and package manifests.

moduleDefinitions.name: External Adapters
moduleDefinitions.responsibility: Provide explicit Git, workspace-filesystem, normalized-extract, optional MATLAB-tooling, source-link, and local-package-export boundaries without leaking credentials or proprietary formats into domain modules.

dependencyEdges.reason: The front end requires current workspace, baseline, revision, sample-state, and refresh-publication context.
The front end renders all lifecycle-area routes and phase-specific projections through one explorer contract.
Search, dossiers, traces, provenance, staleness, and comparison pivots require normalized graph queries.
Findings, reviews, objective mappings, audit packages, and closure actions are exposed as user workflows.
The interface starts refreshes and presents progress, diagnostics, reliability gates, and publication results.
Sample badges, reset controls, seeded walkthroughs, and offline fallback status come from the sample boundary.
Workspace selection resolves the built-in sample only when no real project is configured and retains it under Examples afterward.
Project definitions, exact revision sets, baselines, snapshot identities, selection history, and publication state require persistence.
Real-project revision resolution and source navigation require Git and workspace boundaries.
Each lifecycle projection is scoped to the selected project, snapshot, baseline, and comparison target.
Phase tables, readiness metrics, evidence flow, and compare views are derived from normalized artifacts and relationships.
Every lifecycle area presents contextual reviews, findings, objective mappings, and package membership.
Artifacts, relationships, provenance, hashes, search indexes, staleness inputs, and trace projections are stored behind one persistence boundary.
Findings, reviews, objectives, and package items must reference canonical evidence identities and revisions.
Workflow state, immutable histories, review records, mappings, and package manifests require durable storage.
Audit package export writes only deterministic local artifacts through a dedicated outbound port.
A refresh must validate configuration and freeze an exact multi-repository revision set before extraction.
Normalized extractor output and deterministic relationship checks must conform to the canonical graph model.
Deterministic rule results become snapshot-scoped findings while preserving human workflow history.
Candidate evidence, diagnostics, progress, and publication status must be stored atomically.
Real refreshes need Git, filesystem, normalized-extract, and optional licensed MATLAB integration ports.
Bundled normalized sample evidence and resettable overlay state use the same canonical persistence and query contracts as real projects.

operationAllocations.reason: This module owns the complete user-facing route and interaction shell.
Selection, fallback, exact revision sets, and baseline identity are workspace rules.
Phase-specific navigation, subviews, metrics, tables, and compare projections belong to the explorer model.
A dossier is a canonical graph projection around one artifact identity and revision.
Typed bidirectional traversal and change impact are graph responsibilities.
Cross-phase search resolves canonical artifacts and relationships.
Assignment, corrective action, reverification, closure, and history are assurance business rules.
Review scope, independence, comments, decisions, and evidence links belong to assurance.
Package selection, manifest, hashes, revision metadata, and watermark enforcement form one assurance workflow.
Scanning, extraction, normalization, checks, diagnostics, and atomic publication are one coordinated process.
The deterministic fallback and sample identity are owned by the sample module.
Only the sample module knows bundled defaults and reset semantics.
Durable canonical and overlay state is owned by the persistence module.
All technology-specific reads, source links, revision operations, and exports cross explicit adapter ports.

adapterAllocations.reason: Resolve exact commits, inspect configured repositories, and construct optional company-controlled source links.
Read configured artifacts and normalized extracts without writing authoritative sources.
Accept organization-provided normalized manifests for proprietary or unsupported lifecycle formats.
Optionally invoke licensed MATLAB and Simulink extractors when available for real projects.
Write deterministic audit packages to a user-selected local destination with enforced sample watermark metadata.

proposals: Implement as a modular monolith so the medium-sized sample remains simple to run while module contracts stay reusable for real-project adapters.
Use one normalized evidence graph for sample and real projects, with immutable snapshot data separated from editable hub-local workflow overlays.
Keep all nine lifecycle areas in one Lifecycle Explorer module because they share navigation anatomy and projection rules while differing only in tailored phase queries.
Keep findings, reviews, objective mappings, and package assembly together because they share assurance history, attribution, and evidence-reference invariants.
Bundle a normalized synthetic sample snapshot and never counterfeit proprietary MATLAB binary formats.
Keep persistence and external technology boundaries terminal to preserve an acyclic dependency graph.
